steps.title: Target the conclusion
steps.content: The researchers concluded that anatomical features may affect dogs' ability to distinguish speech from nonspeech. Support must therefore connect a physical feature, such as head length, to different responses to actual language versus the scrambled nonspeech recording that does not resemble human speech.

steps.title: Reject wrong comparisons
steps.content: Choice A compares responses to familiar languages but not to nonspeech. Choice C compares mismatched groups and conditions. Choice D concerns familiar versus unfamiliar language, not speech versus nonspeech. The needed finding ties longer heads to a larger speech-nonspeech brain-activity gap across the recordings. The answer is B.
